heading: Responsibilities of furnishers of information to consumer reporting agencies

children.heading: Duty of furnishers of information to provide accurate information
children.label: a Duty of furnishers of information to provide accurate information

children.heading: Reporting information with actual knowledge of errors
children.label: A Reporting information with actual knowledge of errors
children.text: A person shall not furnish any information relating to a consumer to any consumer reporting agency if the person knows or has reasonable cause to believe that the information is inaccurate.

children.heading: Reporting information after notice and confirmation of errors
children.label: B Reporting information after notice and confirmation of errors
i
children.text: the person has been notified by the consumer, at the address specified by the person for such notices, that specific information is inaccurate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the information is, in fact, inaccurate.

children.heading: No address requirement
children.label: C No address requirement
children.text: A person who clearly and conspicuously specifies to the consumer an address for notices referred to in subparagraph (B) shall not be subject to subparagraph (A); however, nothing in subparagraph (B) shall require a person to specify such an address.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: D Definition
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “reasonable cause to believe that the information is inaccurate” means having specific knowledge, other than solely allegations by the consumer, that would cause a reasonable person to have substantial doubts about the accuracy of the information.

children.heading: Rehabilitation of private education loans
children.label: E Rehabilitation of private education loans
I
children.text: the financial institution chooses to offer a loan rehabilitation program which includes, without limitation, a requirement of the consumer to make consecutive on-time monthly payments in a number that demonstrates, in the assessment of the financial institution offering the loan rehabilitation program, a renewed ability and willingness to repay the loan; and

children.label: II
children.text: the requirements of the loan rehabilitation program described in subclause (I) are successfully met.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If a financial institution is supervised by a Federal banking agency, the financial institution shall seek written approval concerning the terms and conditions of the loan rehabilitation program described in clause (i) from the appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.heading: Feedback
children.label: II Feedback
children.text: An appropriate Federal banking agency shall provide feedback to a financial institution within 120 days of a request for approval under subclause (I).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A consumer may obtain the benefits available under this subsection with respect to rehabilitating a loan only 1 time per loan.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: II Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subparagraph may be construed to require a financial institution to offer a loan rehabilitation program or to remove any reported default from a consumer report as a consideration of a loan rehabilitation program, except as described in clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: the term “appropriate Federal banking agency” has the meaning given the term in ; andsection 1813 of title 12

children.label: II
children.text: the term “private education loan” has the meaning given the term in .section 1650(a) of this title

children.heading: Reporting information during COVID–19 pandemic
children.label: F Reporting information during COVID–19 pandemic

children.heading: Accommodation
children.label: I Accommodation
children.text: The term “accommodation” includes an agreement to defer 1 or more payments, make a partial payment, forbear any delinquent amounts, modify a loan or contract, or any other assistance or relief granted to a consumer who is affected by the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID–19) pandemic during the covered period.

children.label: aa
children.text: 120 days after ; orMarch 27, 2020

children.label: bb
children.text: 120 days after the date on which the national emergency concerning the novel coronavirus disease (COVID–19) outbreak declared by the President on under the National Emergencies Act ( et seq.) terminates.March 13, 202050 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: I
children.text: report the credit obligation or account as current; or

children.label: aa
children.text: maintain the delinquent status during the period in which the accommodation is in effect; and

children.label: bb
children.text: if the consumer brings the credit obligation or account current during the period described in item (aa), report the credit obligation or account as current.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: iii Exception
children.text: Clause (ii) shall not apply with respect to a credit obligation or account of a consumer that has been charged-off.

children.heading: Duty to correct and update information
children.label: 2 Duty to correct and update information
A
children.text: regularly and in the ordinary course of business furnishes information to one or more consumer reporting agencies about the person’s transactions or experiences with any consumer; and

children.label: B
children.text: has furnished to a consumer reporting agency information that the person determines is not complete or accurate,

children.heading: Duty to provide notice of dispute
children.label: 3 Duty to provide notice of dispute
children.text: If the completeness or accuracy of any information furnished by any person to any consumer reporting agency is disputed to such person by a consumer, the person may not furnish the information to any consumer reporting agency without notice that such information is disputed by the consumer.

children.heading: Duty to provide notice of closed accounts
children.label: 4 Duty to provide notice of closed accounts
children.text: A person who regularly and in the ordinary course of business furnishes information to a consumer reporting agency regarding a consumer who has a credit account with that person shall notify the agency of the voluntary closure of the account by the consumer, in information regularly furnished for the period in which the account is closed.

children.heading: Duty to provide notice of delinquency of accounts
children.label: 5 Duty to provide notice of delinquency of accounts

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A person who furnishes information to a consumer reporting agency regarding a delinquent account being placed for collection, charged to profit or loss, or subjected to any similar action shall, not later than 90 days after furnishing the information, notify the agency of the date of delinquency on the account, which shall be the month and year of the commencement of the delinquency on the account that immediately preceded the action.

children.label: i
children.text: the person reports the same date of delinquency as that provided by the creditor to which the account was owed at the time at which the commencement of the delinquency occurred, if the creditor previously reported that date of delinquency to a consumer reporting agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: the creditor did not previously report the date of delinquency to a consumer reporting agency, and the person establishes and follows reasonable procedures to obtain the date of delinquency from the creditor or another reliable source and reports that date to a consumer reporting agency as the date of delinquency; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the creditor did not previously report the date of delinquency to a consumer reporting agency and the date of delinquency cannot be reasonably obtained as provided in clause (ii), the person establishes and follows reasonable procedures to ensure the date reported as the date of delinquency precedes the date on which the account is placed for collection, charged to profit or loss, or subjected to any similar action, and reports such date to the credit reporting agency.

children.heading: Duties of furnishers upon notice of identity theft-related information
children.label: 6 Duties of furnishers upon notice of identity theft-related information

children.heading: Reasonable procedures
children.label: A Reasonable procedures
children.text: A person that furnishes information to any consumer reporting agency shall have in place reasonable procedures to respond to any notification that it receives from a consumer reporting agency under relating to information resulting from identity theft, to prevent that person from refurnishing such blocked information.section 1681c–2 of this title

children.heading: Information alleged to result from identity theft
children.label: B Information alleged to result from identity theft
children.text: If a consumer submits an identity theft report to a person who furnishes information to a consumer reporting agency at the address specified by that person for receiving such reports stating that information maintained by such person that purports to relate to the consumer resulted from identity theft, the person may not furnish such information that purports to relate to the consumer to any consumer reporting agency, unless the person subsequently knows or is informed by the consumer that the information is correct.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If any financial institution that extends credit and regularly and in the ordinary course of business furnishes information to a consumer reporting agency described in furnishes negative information to such an agency regarding credit extended to a customer, the financial institution shall provide a notice of such furnishing of negative information, in writing, to the customer.section 1681a(p) of this title

children.heading: Notice effective for subsequent submissions
children.label: ii Notice effective for subsequent submissions
children.text: After providing such notice, the financial institution may submit additional negative information to a consumer reporting agency described in with respect to the same transaction, extension of credit, account, or customer without providing additional notice to the customer.section 1681a(p) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The notice required under subparagraph (A) shall be provided to the customer prior to, or no later than 30 days after, furnishing the negative information to a consumer reporting agency described in .section 1681a(p) of this title

children.heading: Coordination with new account disclosures
children.label: ii Coordination with new account disclosures
children.text: If the notice is provided to the customer prior to furnishing the negative information to a consumer reporting agency, the notice may not be included in the initial disclosures provided under .section 1637(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: may be included on or with any notice of default, any billing statement, or any other materials provided to the customer; and

children.label: ii
children.text: must be clear and conspicuous.

children.heading: Duty of Bureau
children.label: i Duty of Bureau
children.text: The Bureau shall prescribe a brief model disclosure that a financial institution may use to comply with subparagraph (A), which shall not exceed 30 words.

children.heading: Use of model not required
children.label: ii Use of model not required
children.text: No provision of this paragraph may be construed to require a financial institution to use any such model form prescribed by the Bureau.

children.heading: Compliance using model
children.label: iii Compliance using model
children.text: A financial institution shall be deemed to be in compliance with subparagraph (A) if the financial institution uses any model form prescribed by the Bureau under this subparagraph, or the financial institution uses any such model form and rearranges its format.

children.heading: Use of notice without submitting negative information
children.label: E Use of notice without submitting negative information
children.text: No provision of this paragraph shall be construed as requiring a financial institution that has provided a customer with a notice described in subparagraph (A) to furnish negative information about the customer to a consumer reporting agency.

children.heading: Safe harbor
children.label: F Safe harbor
children.text: A financial institution shall not be liable for failure to perform the duties required by this paragraph if, at the time of the failure, the financial institution maintained reasonable policies and procedures to comply with this paragraph or the financial institution reasonably believed that the institution is prohibited, by law, from contacting the consumer.

children.heading: Negative information
children.label: i Negative information
children.text: The term “negative information” means information concerning a customer’s delinquencies, late payments, insolvency, or any form of default.

children.heading: Customer; financial institution
children.label: ii Customer; financial institution
children.text: The terms “customer” and “financial institution” have the same meanings as in .section 6809 of this title

children.heading: Ability of consumer to dispute information directly with furnisher
children.label: 8 Ability of consumer to dispute information directly with furnisher

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Bureau, in consultation with the Federal Trade Commission, the Federal banking agencies, and the National Credit Union Administration, shall prescribe regulations that shall identify the circumstances under which a furnisher shall be required to reinvestigate a dispute concerning the accuracy of information contained in a consumer report on the consumer, based on a direct request of a consumer.

children.label: i
children.text: the benefits to consumers with the costs on furnishers and the credit reporting system;

children.label: ii
children.text: the impact on the overall accuracy and integrity of consumer reports of any such requirements;

children.label: iii
children.text: whether direct contact by the consumer with the furnisher would likely result in the most expeditious resolution of any such dispute; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the potential impact on the credit reporting process if credit repair organizations, as defined in , including entities that would be a credit repair organization, but for , are able to circumvent the prohibition in subparagraph (G).section 1679a(3) of this titlesection 1679a(3)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: C Applicability
children.text: Subparagraphs (D) through (G) shall apply in any circumstance identified under the regulations promulgated under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Submitting a notice of dispute
children.label: D Submitting a notice of dispute
i
children.text: identifies the specific information that is being disputed;

children.label: ii
children.text: explains the basis for the dispute; and

children.label: iii
children.text: includes all supporting documentation required by the furnisher to substantiate the basis of the dispute.

children.heading: Duty of person after receiving notice of dispute
children.label: E Duty of person after receiving notice of dispute
i
children.text: conduct an investigation with respect to the disputed information;

children.label: ii
children.text: review all relevant information provided by the consumer with the notice;

children.label: iii
children.text: complete such person’s investigation of the dispute and report the results of the investigation to the consumer before the expiration of the period under within which a consumer reporting agency would be required to complete its action if the consumer had elected to dispute the information under that section; andsection 1681i(a)(1) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: if the investigation finds that the information reported was inaccurate, promptly notify each consumer reporting agency to which the person furnished the inaccurate information of that determination and provide to the agency any correction to that information that is necessary to make the information provided by the person accurate.

children.label: I
children.text: by reason of the failure of a consumer to provide sufficient information to investigate the disputed information; or

children.label: II
children.text: the submission by a consumer of a dispute that is substantially the same as a dispute previously submitted by or for the consumer, either directly to the person or through a consumer reporting agency under subsection (b), with respect to which the person has already performed the person’s duties under this paragraph or subsection (b), as applicable.

children.heading: Notice of determination
children.label: ii Notice of determination
children.text: Upon making any determination under clause (i) that a dispute is frivolous or irrelevant, the person shall notify the consumer of such determination not later than 5 business days after making such determination, by mail or, if authorized by the consumer for that purpose, by any other means available to the person.

children.label: I
children.text: the reasons for the determination under clause (i); and

children.label: II
children.text: identification of any information required to investigate the disputed information, which may consist of a standardized form describing the general nature of such information.

children.heading: Exclusion of credit repair organizations
children.label: G Exclusion of credit repair organizations
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply if the notice of the dispute is submitted by, is prepared on behalf of the consumer by, or is submitted on a form supplied to the consumer by, a credit repair organization, as defined in , or an entity that would be a credit repair organization, but for .section 1679a(3) of this titlesection 1679a(3)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Duty to provide notice of status as medical information furnisher
children.label: 9 Duty to provide notice of status as medical information furnisher
children.text: A person whose primary business is providing medical services, products, or devices, or the person’s agent or assignee, who furnishes information to a consumer reporting agency on a consumer shall be considered a medical information furnisher for purposes of this subchapter, and shall notify the agency of such status.

children.heading: Duties of furnishers of information upon notice of dispute
children.label: b Duties of furnishers of information upon notice of dispute
A
children.text: conduct an investigation with respect to the disputed information;

children.label: B
children.text: review all relevant information provided by the consumer reporting agency pursuant to ;section 1681i(a)(2) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: report the results of the investigation to the consumer reporting agency;

children.label: D
children.text: if the investigation finds that the information is incomplete or inaccurate, report those results to all other consumer reporting agencies to which the person furnished the information and that compile and maintain files on consumers on a nationwide basis; and

children.label: i
children.text: modify that item of information;

children.label: ii
children.text: delete that item of information; or

children.label: iii
children.text: permanently block the reporting of that item of information.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 2 Deadline
children.text: A person shall complete all investigations, reviews, and reports required under paragraph (1) regarding information provided by the person to a consumer reporting agency, before the expiration of the period under within which the consumer reporting agency is required to complete actions required by that section regarding that information.section 1681i(a)(1) of this title
subsection (a) of this section, including any regulations issued thereunder;

subsection (e) of this section, except that nothing in this paragraph shall limit, expand, or otherwise affect liability under section 1681n or 1681 of this title, as applicable, for violations of subsection (b) of this section; oro
subsection (e) of .section 1681m of this title

children.heading: Limitation on enforcement
children.label: d Limitation on enforcement
children.text: The provisions of law described in paragraphs (1) through (3) of subsection (c) (other than with respect to the exception described in paragraph (2) of subsection (c)) shall be enforced exclusively as provided under by the Federal agencies and officials and the State officials identified in .section 1681s of this titlesection 1681s of this title

children.heading: Accuracy guidelines and regulations required
children.label: e Accuracy guidelines and regulations required
A
children.text: establish and maintain guidelines for use by each person that furnishes information to a consumer reporting agency regarding the accuracy and integrity of the information relating to consumers that such entities furnish to consumer reporting agencies, and update such guidelines as often as necessary; and

children.label: B
children.text: prescribe regulations requiring each person that furnishes information to a consumer reporting agency to establish reasonable policies and procedures for implementing the guidelines established pursuant to subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: identify patterns, practices, and specific forms of activity that can compromise the accuracy and integrity of information furnished to consumer reporting agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: review the methods (including technological means) used to furnish information relating to consumers to consumer reporting agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: determine whether persons that furnish information to consumer reporting agencies maintain and enforce policies to ensure the accuracy and integrity of information furnished to consumer reporting agencies; and

children.label: D
children.text: examine the policies and processes that persons that furnish information to consumer reporting agencies employ to conduct reinvestigations and correct inaccurate information relating to consumers that has been furnished to consumer reporting agencies.
